lesson.menu_desc: Capstone review: the full Washington congress experience integrated -- requests, ordering, directions, invitations, future plans, conditionals and agreement (review of lessons 11-17) -- 29 slides
lesson.subtitle: Aula 18 -- The Confident Communicator (Review & Integration)
lesson.title_tag: Professor View -- Marcos Mansour | Aula 18 -- The Confident Communicator (Review)

listenings.text: What a week in Washington. Let me recap the whole trip. On the first day, I arrived at the airport. I showed my boarding pass and went through immigration without any trouble. At the hotel, I confirmed my reservation and checked in. That evening, I went to a restaurant. I asked the server for the menu, ordered the grilled salmon, and at the end I asked for the bill. The next day, I needed to find the congress center. I stopped a stranger near a famous landmark and asked for directions. He told me to go straight, turn left at the crosswalk, and the building was right there. During the congress, I received an invitation to a networking dinner. I talked about my goals for the future, and I even negotiated a small partnership. By the last day, I felt completely confident. I could handle the whole trip in English.

Hi Marcos, this is Laura from the congress team. I just wanted to say what a pleasure it was to meet you this week. Honestly, your English was so confident. You asked for directions, you handled the dinner invitations, and you spoke about your goals like a true professional. The partnership we discussed sounds very promising. If you send the final terms next week, we will sign right away. I am sure this is the start of a great relationship. Keep practicing, keep speaking, and stay confident. I really look forward to working with you. Have a safe trip home, and congratulations on finishing the whole program.
